summary: Sprint 6 spec patches for ui-feed-list-actions REQ-FEED-030.1 / REQ-FEED-034 contain multiple HIGH-severity defects across spec_fidelity, edge_case_coverage, and implementation_correctness. Critical issues: (1) REQ-FEED-034 misstates DOM hierarchy by calling `.block-editor-surface` a sibling of `.row-button` when current FeedRow.svelte:322-471 places it as sibling of `.row-layout` (FIND-S6-SPEC-001); (2) REQ-FEED-030.1 EARS conflates data-testid attributes with class names creating ambiguity for Phase 2a test authors (FIND-S6-SPEC-002); (3) the `shouldMountBlocks=true AND blockEditorAdapter=null` race produces a blank row with no preview and no block-element — not covered by any AC or PROP (FIND-S6-SPEC-003); (4) PROP-FEED-S6-003 grep regex `opacity:\s*0` over-matches existing fractional opacity values used in box-shadow rgba and risks false positives (FIND-S6-SPEC-005); (5) Phase 2 Red-phase gate lists PROP-FEED-S6-006 as a failing test pre-impl, but Sprint 5 DOM hierarchy already makes that PROP PASS, breaking TDD invariant (FIND-S6-SPEC-006). Additional MEDIUM/LOW findings cover delete-button DOM-presence not asserted, fast-check arbitrary under-specification, transition-race coverage gap for EC-FEED-021, redundant PROP overlap between S6-001/S6-005, Phase 2 gate wording inconsistency, and missing fallback-blocks=0 cross-reference in truth table.
